evals.name: canonical-post-no-linear
evals.prompt:
I just got a panel-review back on PR 85 in catena-labs/example-repo (head SHA: abc123def456). Here are the findings:

Codex (MEDIUM, security)
- apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx:240 — External OAuth callbacks redirect to /v1/auth/oauth2/authorize while the post-signup onboarding gate is active. A new CLI/MCP magic-link signup can receive an access token without completing KYB.
  Fix: defer the non-console OAuth redirect until postSignupOnboardingActive is false.

Claude (LOW)
- apps/api/src/routes/organization.ts:33 — maxLength(80) runs against pre-trim string; trim should be inside the validator pipe.

Opencode (MEDIUM, deployment)
- apps/api/src/services/sign-up-provisioning.ts:37 — Drop of NODE_ENV === "production" from the BRIDGE_LIVE guard shifts behavior in staging.
  Fix: confirm staging keeps BRIDGE_LIVE=false or seed customer ID pre-KYB.

Linear is NOT reachable from this session (no Linear MCP, no Linear CLI, no API token). Walk me through the triage. Dry run: do NOT call gh api. Write the per-comment payloads to ./comments.json and the triage transcript to ./triage_transcript.md. My Stage 1 answer: post all three.
evals.expected_output: comments.json is an array of exactly 3 per-comment payloads (one per finding), each formatted as it would be POSTed to `/repos/catena-labs/example-repo/pulls/85/comments` — i.e. each object has `body`, `commit_id: "abc123def456"`, `path`, and `line` fields. **There is NO top-level review wrapper, NO `event: COMMENT`, NO `comments[]` array nested inside a review object** — it's a flat array of independent comment payloads. **Each `body` contains NO severity, NO `Recommendation:`/`Must fix`/`Should fix` line, NO priority, and NO panelist/agent attribution (no "Codex", "Claude", "Opencode", no `<sub>` footer).** Each body is just the finding text verbatim, followed (when the finding had a suggested fix) by a `**Possible Solution:**` line. So: the codex MEDIUM (auth callback) body is the finding text + `**Possible Solution:** defer the non-console OAuth redirect until postSignupOnboardingActive is false.`; the opencode MEDIUM (sign-up provisioning) body is the finding text + `**Possible Solution:** confirm staging keeps BRIDGE_LIVE=false or seed customer ID pre-KYB.`; the claude LOW (maxLength trim) body has NO Possible Solution line (none was given) and, because it is a LOW/polish finding, its body is prefixed with `Small / Optional polish:` (or equivalent soft, clearly-optional phrasing). Order in comments.json is HIGH→MEDIUM→LOW: codex MEDIUM and opencode MEDIUM appear before claude LOW. triage_transcript.md should: (a) include the full numbered finding list as it would have been printed in chat, with a plain-URL GitHub diff-view deep-link per finding in the form `https://github.com/catena-labs/example-repo/pull/85/files#diff-<sha256(path)>R<line>`, (b) record the exact AskUserQuestion option labels for Stage 1 (terse `#N: <panelist> <severity> <path>:<line>` form — severity may appear in the triage option labels even though it never appears in the posted comment), and (c) record that Stage 2 was SKIPPED because Linear is not reachable from this session — not that the user declined it, but that it was never asked. No `./linear_tickets.json` should be written. The three findings should all be marked 'posted to PR' in the transcript.

evals.name: mixed-routing-with-linear
evals.prompt:
I have panel-review findings for PR 156 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: 4d8c1f9). Linear IS reachable from this session (the agent has a working path to create Linear issues — MCP, CLI, or API). I want to route findings: HIGHs to the PR, MEDIUMs to Linear tickets, LOWs dropped entirely. For Linear, file tickets under team "Platform" project "Code Health".

Findings:

Codex (HIGH, security)
- apps/api/src/auth/jwt.ts:45 — JWT verification skipped when ENV.SKIP_AUTH=true; this flag exists in production env vars.
  Fix: remove the SKIP_AUTH bypass entirely or gate it on NODE_ENV !== 'production'.

Claude (MEDIUM)
- apps/web/src/components/Button.tsx:12 — Loading state has no aria-busy; screen readers won't announce the disabled state during async actions.

Opencode (MEDIUM, performance)
- apps/api/src/services/search.ts:201 — Sequential await in a loop over `users`; should be Promise.all if the calls are independent.
  Fix: Promise.all(users.map(getProfile)).

Claude (LOW)
- apps/web/src/utils/format.ts:7 — Date formatter doesn't handle null input.

Dry run: comments to ./comments.json, linear tickets to ./linear_tickets.json, transcript to ./triage_transcript.md. Don't actually call gh api or the Linear MCP.
evals.expected_output: comments.json is an array of exactly 1 per-comment payload (the codex HIGH at jwt.ts:45), each object having `body`, `commit_id: "4d8c1f9"`, `path: "apps/api/src/auth/jwt.ts"`, and `line: 45`. **Flat array, no review wrapper.** The body is the finding text verbatim followed by `**Possible Solution:** remove the SKIP_AUTH bypass entirely or gate it on NODE_ENV !== 'production'.` — and **NOTHING else**: no severity, no `Recommendation:`/`Must fix` line, no priority, no panelist/agent attribution, no `<sub>` footer. It is a HIGH finding, so there is NO `Small / Optional polish:` prefix (that prefix is only for LOW/polish findings). linear_tickets.json has exactly 2 ticket objects, each with `team: "Platform"`, `project: "Code Health"`, a `title` derived from the finding's first sentence (~80 chars max), and a `description` that contains in order: the PR link with the file:line deep-link, `File: apps/web/src/components/Button.tsx:12` (or the opencode path/line), `Severity: Medium`, a blank line, then the finding body, then the fix if any. **Tickets must NOT have a `priority` field set** (or it must be unset/null) — the skill never maps severity to Linear priority. The LOW finding (format.ts) must NOT appear in either file — it's dropped. The skill must only ask the user for team/project ONCE upfront, not per-finding. triage_transcript.md lists all 4 findings with dispositions: 1 'posted to PR', 2 'filed to Linear', 1 'dropped'. The Stage 1 and Stage 2 AskUserQuestion option labels must be recorded, both in the terse `#N: <panelist> <severity> <path>:<line>` form. Stage 2 must only have shown the 3 leftovers (not the HIGH that was already selected in Stage 1).

evals.name: finding-outside-diff-hunk
evals.prompt:
Post these panel-review findings to PR 27 in catena-labs/api-service (head SHA: 9c4b8e2). Linear is NOT reachable from this session. Dry-run mode — write to ./comments.json and ./triage_transcript.md, do not call gh api.

Codex (HIGH, security)
- src/auth/session.ts:142 — Session cookie missing SameSite=Strict; CSRF risk on the /admin endpoints.

Claude (MEDIUM)
- src/utils/logger.ts:8 — Logger swallows errors silently; should at least console.warn on serialization failure.

IMPORTANT context for the skill: src/utils/logger.ts:8 is on a line that is NOT part of the PR diff — it's pre-existing code that this PR didn't touch. The codex finding at src/auth/session.ts:142 IS on a changed line. GitHub will reject inline comments on lines outside the diff hunk. Please handle this correctly. My Stage 1 answer: post both.
evals.expected_output: comments.json contains BOTH per-comment payloads as a flat array (the skill cannot know in advance which lines GitHub will reject — it sends each and lets the API tell it). Each object has `body`, `commit_id: "9c4b8e2"`, `path`, and `line`. Each body is the finding prose verbatim and **nothing else**: NO severity, NO `Recommendation:`/`Must fix`/`Should fix` line, NO priority, NO panelist/agent attribution, NO `<sub>` footer. Neither finding supplied a suggested fix, so **neither body has a `**Possible Solution:**` line** (the skill must not invent one). Both findings are HIGH/MEDIUM, so neither gets the `Small / Optional polish:` prefix. **No review wrapper in the JSON structure.** The codex HIGH finding must come first in the array (severity ordering — HIGH before MEDIUM). triage_transcript.md should explicitly call out that src/utils/logger.ts:8 is likely to be rejected because it's outside the diff hunk, and state that on a real (non-dry-run) run the skill would AUTOMATICALLY repost it as a top-level PR issue comment (via `POST /issues/{N}/comments` / `gh pr comment`) carrying the `file:line` and blob deep-link in a `**Location:**` line in the body — NOT recommend the user do it manually, and NOT drop it. Deep-link rules in the transcript: the codex finding (changed line) gets a /pull/N/files#diff-<sha256(path)>R<line> diff-view link; the claude finding (outside hunk) gets the blob fallback `https://github.com/catena-labs/api-service/blob/9c4b8e2/src/utils/logger.ts#L8`. The skill must NOT silently drop the logger.ts finding before posting. Stage 2 must be marked SKIPPED in the transcript because Linear isn't reachable.

evals.name: missing-pr-ref
evals.prompt:
I just got panel-review findings back. Please post them to GitHub. Findings:

Codex (MEDIUM)
- packages/db/src/migrations/0042_add_audit.sql:12 — NOT NULL added without a backfill default; existing rows will fail the migration.
  Fix: add DEFAULT now() or run a backfill in a separate migration first.

Claude (LOW)
- packages/db/src/schema.ts:88 — auditLog table defined but no index on user_id; queries by user will scan.

Dry-run mode: write to ./comments.json and ./triage_transcript.md. My Stage 1 answer (when you get to triaging): post both.
evals.expected_output: The skill MUST NOT proceed to triage without first asking the user for the PR number or URL. There's no PR ref in the prompt and the skill's own guidance forbids auto-detecting from the current branch. Expected behavior: a clarifying question (asked once, ideally via AskUserQuestion or as a direct question in chat) requesting the PR ref. comments.json should NOT exist yet (or should exist but be empty / clearly marked 'awaiting PR ref'). The skill should NOT have run `gh pr view` against an inferred branch and should NOT have constructed any payloads guessing at the repo. If the skill produced comments.json anyway, that's a fail.

evals.name: drop-the-leftovers
evals.prompt:
Triage these panel-review findings for PR 412 in catena-labs/payments-service (head SHA: 7f2e1a). Linear IS reachable from this session. Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./linear_tickets.json, ./triage_transcript.md. Don't actually call any APIs.

Findings:

Codex (HIGH, security)
- src/charges/idempotency.ts:42 — Idempotency key derived from request timestamp; concurrent retries within the same millisecond will double-charge.
  Fix: derive the key from request body hash.

Claude (MEDIUM)
- src/charges/refund.ts:88 — Refund amount validation allows negative values; will create a charge instead of a refund.

Opencode (LOW)
- src/charges/log.ts:7 — Log line missing the customer ID; harder to grep for support tickets.

My Stage 1 answer: post just the codex HIGH. My Stage 2 answer: file just the claude MEDIUM in Linear team "Payments" project "Bugs". The opencode LOW: I don't pick it in either stage.
evals.expected_output: comments.json is a flat array with exactly 1 per-comment payload (codex HIGH at idempotency.ts:42), with `commit_id: "7f2e1a"`. Its body is the finding text verbatim + `**Possible Solution:** derive the key from request body hash.` and **nothing else** — no severity, no `Recommendation:` line, no priority, no panelist/agent attribution, no `<sub>` footer, and (being a HIGH) no `Small / Optional polish:` prefix. linear_tickets.json has exactly 1 ticket (the claude MEDIUM about refund.ts:88) with `team: "Payments"`, `project: "Bugs"`, no `priority`. The opencode LOW (log.ts:7) must NOT appear in either output — it's dropped. triage_transcript.md must explicitly mark the opencode LOW as 'dropped' (not 'skipped', not 'declined') — the user did not pick it in either Stage 1 or Stage 2, so it's dropped per the skill's flow. The Stage 1 AskUserQuestion option labels must show all 3 findings; the Stage 2 option labels must show only the 2 leftovers (NOT the codex HIGH that was already picked in Stage 1). The skill must have asked the user for team/project once before filing — recorded in the transcript. The transcript should also include a 'Posting 1, filing 1, dropping 1' confirmation line shown to the user before action.

evals.name: many-findings-chunked-select
evals.prompt:
Triage these 7 panel-review findings for PR 200 in catena-labs/api-service (head SHA: deadbeefcafe). Linear is NOT reachable. Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./triage_transcript.md, no gh api.

Findings:

1. Codex (HIGH, security) - src/auth/login.ts:30 — SQL string concatenation in WHERE clause; SQL injection.
2. Codex (HIGH, correctness) - src/billing/charge.ts:88 — Race condition in concurrent charge.
3. Claude (MEDIUM) - src/api/users.ts:45 — No rate limit on /users endpoint.
4. Claude (MEDIUM) - src/api/orders.ts:60 — Missing input validation on quantity field.
5. Opencode (MEDIUM) - src/utils/cache.ts:22 — Cache key collision possible with similar inputs.
6. Claude (LOW) - src/utils/format.ts:5 — Date format hardcoded to en-US.
7. Opencode (LOW) - src/api/health.ts:3 — Health check doesn't verify DB connection.

My Stage 1 answer: post #1, #2, #3, #4 (both HIGHs and the first two MEDIUMs). Don't post the rest.
evals.expected_output: comments.json is a flat array with exactly 4 per-comment payloads, in HIGH→MEDIUM order: #1 (codex login.ts), #2 (codex charge.ts), #3 (claude users.ts), #4 (claude orders.ts). All with `commit_id: "deadbeefcafe"`. Each body is the finding prose verbatim with NO severity, NO `Recommendation:` line, NO priority, and NO panelist/agent attribution; none of these findings supplied a fix line, so none has a `**Possible Solution:**` line; all four are HIGH/MEDIUM so none gets the `Small / Optional polish:` prefix. **The skill must have chunked the Stage 1 multi-select** because there are 7 findings, exceeding the AskUserQuestion 4-options-per-question limit. The transcript must show the chunking clearly: either (a) one AskUserQuestion call with multiple `questions[]` entries grouped by severity (e.g. one question for the 2 HIGHs, one for the 3 MEDIUMs, one for the 2 LOWs), or (b) sequential AskUserQuestion calls covering chunks of ≤4 findings each. Each chunk's option labels use the terse `#N: <panelist> <severity> <path>:<line>` form. Stage 2 must be marked SKIPPED in the transcript because Linear is not reachable. Findings #5, #6, #7 must be marked 'dropped' in the transcript (the user did not pick them in Stage 1, and there was no Stage 2). The pre-action confirmation line should read approximately 'Posting 4, filing 0, dropping 3 — go?'.

evals.name: mergeable-suggestion-vs-prose
evals.prompt:
Post these panel-review findings to PR 310 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: ab12cd34). Linear is NOT reachable. Dry-run: write ./comments.json and ./triage_transcript.md, do not call gh api.

Codex (MEDIUM, correctness)
- apps/api/src/services/search.ts:201 — `parseInt(req.query.limit)` is called without a radix, so a query like `limit=0x10` parses unexpectedly. The single line `  const limit = parseInt(req.query.limit)` (2-space indent) should pass radix 10: `  const limit = parseInt(req.query.limit, 10)`. This is a self-contained one-line replacement — no surrounding lines change.
  Fix: replace that line with `  const limit = parseInt(req.query.limit, 10)`.

Claude (MEDIUM, correctness)
- apps/api/src/services/search.ts:88-90 — The 3-line `users.forEach(u => { results.push(transform(u)) })` block (lines 88, 89, 90, all 2-space indent) mutates a shared array in a loop; replace the whole block with a single immutable map. The exact replacement for those three lines is:
  line 88: `  const results = users.map(transform)`
  (lines 89 and 90 are removed by the replacement).
  Fix: replace lines 88-90 with the single line `  const results = users.map(transform)`.

Opencode (MEDIUM, architecture)
- apps/api/src/services/search.ts:60 — The search service reaches into the DB layer directly instead of going through the repository abstraction used everywhere else; this couples the service to the schema.
  Fix: route the query through the existing SearchRepository rather than the raw db client.

Context: search.ts:201, :88-90, and :60 are all changed lines inside the PR diff. My Stage 1 answer: post all three.
evals.expected_output: comments.json is a flat array with exactly 3 per-comment payloads (no review wrapper), all with `commit_id: "ab12cd34"` and `path: "apps/api/src/services/search.ts"`. **(1) The codex finding (line 201) is a clean self-contained single-line replacement, so its body MUST carry a mergeable GitHub suggestion: a fenced ```suggestion code block whose contents are exactly `  const limit = parseInt(req.query.limit, 10)` (preserving the 2-space leading indentation), on a single-line comment anchored to `line: 201` (with `side: "RIGHT"`) so GitHub replaces that one line. (2) The claude finding (lines 88-90) is a clean MULTI-LINE drop-in, so its body MUST carry a ```suggestion block whose contents are exactly the single line `  const results = users.map(transform)` (2-space indent), on a MULTI-LINE comment whose targeting is `start_line: 88`, `start_side: "RIGHT"`, `line: 90`, `side: "RIGHT"` — i.e. the anchored range is the full 88..90 span being replaced (`line` is the END of the range, 90, not the start). The suggestion collapsing 3 lines to 1 is valid because the whole anchored span is replaced.** Neither suggestion-bearing body may ALSO contain a `**Possible Solution:**` prose line — suggestion XOR prose. **(3) The opencode finding (line 60) is a conceptual/architectural fix that is NOT a faithful line drop-in, so its body MUST use a `**Possible Solution:**` prose line (route the query through the existing SearchRepository …) and MUST NOT contain a ```suggestion block.** All three bodies are the finding prose verbatim with NO severity, NO `Recommendation:` line, NO priority, NO panelist/agent attribution; all are MEDIUM so none gets the `Small / Optional polish:` prefix. Each suggestion's anchored range must still include the finding's reported location (line 201 stays on 201; the 88-90 finding stays anchored to 88..90) — the anchor is NOT moved off the triaged line. triage_transcript.md must record all three as 'posted to PR' and note for each whether it was posted as a single-line mergeable suggestion, a multi-line mergeable suggestion, or a prose Possible Solution. Stage 2 marked SKIPPED (Linear not reachable).
